description_long: This object is a rugged, military-style projector with a robust, boxy housing primarily in dark green and black. The front features a large display screen with a green radar-like interface, surrounded by a thick bezel and a coiled cable attached to the side, suggesting a handheld or portable use. The sides are equipped with multiple control knobs, switches, and a prominent handle on top for easy carrying. There are several warning and information labels, as well as a visible power switch and what appears to be a lens or sensor module protruding from one side. The base is wide and stable, likely designed for mounting or placement on flat surfaces, and the overall construction appears to be metal with some plastic components. The object is detailed with vents and ridges for heat dissipation, and the design suggests it is built for durability in urban or field environments.
description: A rugged, military-style projector with a large display, control knobs, and a sturdy handle, designed for portable or field use. Its robust construction features dark green and black materials, with visible vents and a coiled cable.
description_view_0: Side view showing the protruding lens module, two control knobs, and a yellow label near the base.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the large green radar display, thick bezel, and coiled cable attached to the right.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a visible power switch, handle, and additional labeling, as well as the coiled cable connection.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the lens module, cable entry, and a mostly featureless dark panel.
attribute_lens_type: fixed; medium-sized cylindrical lens module protruding from the side, no visible adjustment rings
attribute_control_panel_design: The control panel is located on the side and front, featuring two large rotary knobs, a power switch, and several labeled buttons. The display screen is surrounded by a bezel with additional interface markings.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: Vents are present as horizontal slats on the upper side panels, with a ribbed pattern for airflow and heat dissipation.
